rows.Event Name: HER: Health Empowerment Revolution - Phoenix
rows.Event Description: Conference designed for women navigating life with migraine in addition to other chronic health conditions Welcome to the Research shows that migraine frequently coexists with a range of chronic diseases, including asthma, other pain conditions, depression, anxiety, autoimmune conditions, sleep disorders, and more. This overlapping burden means women are managing not only the debilitating symptoms of migraine, but also multiple other health challenges that impact their quality of life. This becomes even more confusing because all women will experience perimenopause and menopause, and the complication of hormones and migraine is overwhelming. Despite this complexity, most women do not receive coordinated or comprehensive care. These conditions are often treated in isolation, each one addressed by a different specialist or clinic, with little to no integration. Primary care providers, though well-intentioned, are often too overwhelmed by patient volume and administrative demands to provide the coordination and continuity women need across their health conditions. To make matters worse, women are typically caregivers in their families and often work full-time. Many of the women in our community report having three or more chronic conditions—and still being expected to manage work, caregiving, and household responsibilities. They simply do not have the time or energy to navigate a fragmented and confusing healthcare system on their own. Don’t miss this opportunity to learn, connect, and take charge of your health. You will leave this event with Dr. Stanford received her BS and MPH from Emory University as an MLK Scholar, her MD from the Medical College of Georgia School of Medicine as a Stoney Scholar, her MPA from the Harvard Kennedy School of Government as a Zuckerman Fellow in the Harvard Center for Public Leadership and her executive MBA as a merit-based scholarship recipient from the Quantic School of Business and Technology. She completed her Obesity Medicine & Nutrition Fellowship at MGH/HMS after completing her internal medicine and pediatrics residency at the University of South Carolina. She has served as a health communications fellow at the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention and as a behavioral sciences intern at the American Cancer Society. Upon completing her MPH, she received the Gold Congressional Award, the highest honor Congress bestows upon America’s youth. Dr. Stanford has completed a medicine and media internship at the Discovery Channel. An American Medical Association (AMA) Foundation Leadership Award recipient in 2005 and an AMA Paul Ambrose Award for national leadership among resident physicians in 2009, she was selected for the AMA Inspirational Physician Award in 2015. The American College of Physicians (ACP) selected her as the 2013 Joseph E. Johnson Leadership Award recipient, and the Massachusetts ACP selected her for the Young Leadership Award in 2015. She is the 2017 recipient of the HMS Amos Diversity Award and the Massachusetts Medical Society (MMS) Award for Women’s Health. In 2019, she was selected as the Suffolk District Community Clinician of the Year for the Reducing Health Disparities Award for MMS. She was chosen for The Obesity Society Clinician of the Year in 2020. In 2021, she was awarded the MMS Grant Rodkey Award for her dedication to medical students and the AMA A board-certified internal medicine physician, Dr. David completed her M.D. degree at the University of Missouri-Kansas City School of Medicine. She completed an internal medicine residency at Banner University Medical Center in Phoenix, Arizona. She is currently a Menopause Society Certified Menopause Practitioner. Dr. David provides consultative care in women's health issues including menopause, sexual health, contraception, as well as hormones and headaches. She is involved in education and is the Director of FRAMES (Foundations of Reasoning and Application in Medicine and Science) at Mayo Clinic Alix School of Medicine Arizona Campus. She is also the Chair of Student Engagement for IGNITEMed, a non-profit organization supporting women medical students. She graduated from Bowdoin College, received her Medical Degree from Jefferson Medical College, and completed an internship and residency in Internal Medicine at Boston University Medical Center (BUMC), where she received the R. Knight Steele Award for Geriatric Medicine. She completed a fellowship in General Internal Medicine and a residency in Preventive Medicine, also at BUMC, as well as a fellowship in Integrative Medicine at the University of Arizona Andrew Weil Center for Integrative Medicine. Dr. Halker Singh sees firsthand how migraine affects women across their lives – from hormonal transitions to mental health challenges and overlapping pain conditions. She is committed to translating complex medical science into clear, practical guidance so patients can better understand their symptoms and feel confident navigating treatment decisions. She serves in national leadership roles within the American Headache Society and the American Academy of Neurology and is Deputy Editor of Her work has been recognized with multiple national awards for service, education, and leadership. Beyond research and publications, what drives Dr. Halker Singh most is empowering patients with knowledge, practical strategies, and hope. At the heart of her work is a simple belief: migraine is a complex, real, and often invisible disease – and people living with it deserve to be heard, supported, and offered thoughtful, evidence-based care that addresses the whole person. Dr. Starling received her M.D. degree from Drexel University College of Medicine in Philadelphia. She completed a Transitional Year Residency, a Neurology Residency, and a Headache Fellowship at the Mayo Clinic College of Medicine in Scottsdale, Arizona. She was also the External Chief Resident for the Department of Neurology, Mayo Clinic, Arizona. She is a Fellow of the American Academy of Neurology (AAN) and the American Headache Society (AHS). She is active in both organizations. In addition, she has served as the Co-Chair of the American Headache and Migraine Association, Chair of the New Investigator and Trainee Special Interest Section of the AHS. She has been an invited speaker to many international, national, and regional meetings and has been a perennial speaker at the AHS Scottsdale Symposium in Scottsdale, Arizona. Her many honors include the AHS Above and Beyond Award for Service, Manfred D. Muenter Award for Excellence in Clinical Neurology, the AAN Annual Meeting Residency Scholarship, the 2012 Spirit of Mayo Clinic Award, and the Mayo Brothers Distinguished Fellowship Award. Dr. Starling’s most recent accolades include the Women Leading in Neurology recognition from the AAN, Super Supporter Award from Miles for Migraine, and the Outstanding Emerging Educator from the Mayo Clinic College of Medicine and Science. Dr. Starling has over 80 peer-reviewed publications and abstracts related to her fields of interest which include migraine, concussion, posttraumatic headache, telemedicine, and graduate medical education. She is an active participant and member of many educational and departmental committees within Mayo Clinic. Dr. Townsend has authored/co-authored over 35 manuscripts and book chapters – the majority focused on chronic pain interdisciplinary rehabilitation utilizing cognitive-behavioral approaches. She served as a consultant for the AZ DHS Pain and Addiction statewide medical school curriculum development. The American Pain Society, Pain Rehabilitation Special Interest Group (2014) awarded her a ‘Meritorious Award for Contributions to the Field’, an acknowledgement by her pain peers for “Outstanding contributions to the field of pain rehabilitation and to the interdisciplinary development of colleagues.” Dr. Townsend’s career highlights include her work as a co-investigator on a National Institute of Health grant held jointly with the Lake Superior Fond du Lac Ojibwe and Mayo Clinic to develop a pain rehabilitation program for Native Americans with chronic pain. During over 25 years in her field of expertise, Dr. Townsend is also engaged in teaching non-interventional/non-pharmacological pain management and comprehensive wellness strategies to medical school, resident, and fellowship trainees across numerous medical disciplines at Mayo Clinic and professional conferences. Dr. Townsend is passionate about normalizing and expanding wellness strategies emphasizing emotional awareness, emotional literacy and well-being into personal and professional conversations. Dr. Townsend’s personal highlights include being inspired by her 4 children, beloved by her tribe of sister-cousins, learning how to play the steel tongue drum, and gardening in the desert. Dr Shihab is the Mayo Clinic, AZ Women’s Health Internal Medicine Division Associate Chair of research with research interests in menopause and implications of social and medical factors on various health processes in women. She is an Assistant Professor of Medicine at the Mayo Clinic College of Medicine and Science and is a faculty at the Women’s Health Fellowship program at Mayo Clinic, AZ. Shirley has worked in healthcare since 1983 and has served on various non-profit boards since 1991. She won’t retire until a cure is found for this disabling disease.

rows.Event Name: Only a Few Seats Left for ITIL 4 in Phoenix, AZ – Register Today
rows.Event Description: ITIL Foundation provides a foundational understanding of the ITIL framework for IT service management, offering guidance and best practices. The Foundation level is the entry level certification and offers you a general awareness of the key elements, concepts, and terminology used in the ITIL® service lifecycle, including the links between lifecycle stages, the processes used and their contribution to service management practices.Learners Ink ITIL® Foundation Exam Prep is designed and developed by the global panel of ITIL® Expert instructors to ensure that our participants pass the ITIL® Foundation exam on their first attempt. Our hands-on training helped many participants in transforming their careers. Our Lead Instructor focus on the key concepts, terminologies and elements included with ITIL® service lifecycle management. The training includes best practices from ITSM processes and Service Management, ITIL® lifecycle Phase Interactions & Outcomes. Service Design: Design Coordination, Service Catalog Management, Service Level Management, Availability Management, Capacity Management, IT Service Continuity Management, Information Security Management. Service Transition: Transition Planning & Support, Change Management, Service Asset & Configuration Management, Release & Deployment Management, Service Validation & Testing, Change Evaluation, Knowledge Management Service Operation: Event Management, Incident Management, Request Fulfilment, Problem Management, Access Management, and the functions of: IT Operations, Technical Management, Application Management, Service Desk Continual Service Improvement: Seven-Step Improvement Process ITIL 4 contains seven guiding principles that were adopted from the most recent ITIL Practitioner Exam, which covers organizational change management, communication, and measurement and metrics. These principles include: · Focus on value · Start where you are · Progress iteratively with feedback · Collaborate and promote visibility · Think and work holistically · Keep it simple and practical · Optimize and automate ITIL 4 focuses on company culture and integrating IT into the overall business structure. It encourages collaboration between IT and other departments, especially as other business units increasingly rely on technology to get work done. Courseware Approved by PEOPLECERT16 hours of instructor-led training (for Traditional classroom and Online Classroom Training) Industry-based case studies & real world-examples Chapter end quizzes & 2 simulation exams 100% Money Back Guarantee* Exam fee included (Exam fee not included for Online Self Learning) 24/7 support via email, online chat, telephone · Manage risk in line with business & service disruption or failure · Show value for money · Optimize customer experience · Enable business change · Aids continuous service improvement · Support business outcomes · The salary ranges from $81,040 to $111,053 per year There is no defined eligibility criterion for ITIL Foundation exam. In order to pass the exam, you will need to get 28 of the 40 questions right, or score a 70%. This means you can get up to 12 questions wrong and still pass. rows.Event Name: Composition in the Field - Photography Workshop
rows.Event Description: Photography Workshop lead by Professional Photographer Each You will have fun in the 2 hour session, learn to shoot strategically, get more interesting photos of the event, and walk away with a foundation to improve your photographs in the future, whether you’re traveling, or at a birthday party and everywhere in between. Entry is free but any photo enthusiast would be willing to pay dearly for the chance to capture the photo ops at the Arizona State University Museum. Start from the dramatic exterior which seems to channel an Indiana Jones movie, with powerful architectural lines that liken the Aztec culture, allowing your Digital Photo Academy Instructor to demonstrate and supervise repeating patterns of blended triangles, squares and other geometric shapes. Compare the visual options from a close up perspective, with the building showcased entirely and no sky or foreground within the frame to half earth, half sky for another energy. Step back and climb the entry steps to offer a panoramic scene with stone balconies, palm trees and towering columns. Wait for the natural light, and long shadows can allow you to depict a mood-laden narrative in which your viewer can lose themselves. Or include one of the oversized outdoor sculptures which can be juxtaposed against the building to catch a more whimsical kind of image. Practice the art of long exposure with the fountains and recreate the impressionistic look of a velvety smooth depiction of flowing water. Upon entering the museum you will come upon cavernous spaces with gleaming light ceilings from high above, rebounding off of the patterned tongue and groove flooring that goes on and on. Accentuate the rows of paintings on the walls with a Leading Lines composition technique. Include any number of indoor sculptures into a single collection sending the eye of your viewer from the lower corner of the frame of your photo up, diagonally, and back for an invigorating visual interaction to the scene you create. There will also be time to wander on the campus itself and your DPA instructor will demonstrate the art of desert landscape photography. Learn Panning as a skateboarder or a bicyclist zips by. Meet your Instructor by the entrance to the museum but make sure to make time to photograph the gigantic stone cowboy boots before the session is over. SE Corner of Mill Avenue and 10th Street51 E. 10th St. Complimentary parking is available in front of the ASU Art Museum in Short-term paid parking is available in the 10th Street Parking Garage across the street from the museum and in

rows.Event Name: LIVE: CO2 QuietNite Laser Therapy
rows.Event Description: Fractional CO2 ablation of the soft palate and oropharyngeal walls has changed the way we manage and reduce snoring. Lasers have proven to improve the outcomes in many aspects of oral health. The advancement of different delivery methods of laser energy have made outcomes more affective and efficient. Fractional CO2 ablation of the soft palate and oropharyngeal walls has changed the way we manage and reduce snoring. Treatments with QuietNite therapy have shown to not only decrease snoring, but also improve the nasal/oropharyngeal connection and allow a significant improvement in airflow. The immediate change via neo-collagenesis of the oropharyngeal wall reduces resistance and improves airflow with long lasting improvements. CO2 laser energy is extremely versatile in the treatment of soft issue therapy ranging from biopsies, surgical excisions, frenectomies and tongue tie release procedures. Most anything soft tissue can have an improved outcome when utilizing CO2 laser techniques.

rows.Event Name: LIVE: Nd:YAG HILT TMD & Implant Care
rows.Event Description: Reducing Orofacial Pain and Improving Joint Health Using DEKA HILT & HILT laser therapy for regeneration and pain management Lasers have proven to improve the outcomes in many aspects of oral health. The advancement of different delivery methods of laser energy have made outcomes more affective and efficient. The DEKA SmartPerio HILT laser is leading the charge in improving treatment outcomes in orofacial pain. The optimization of High Intensity Laser Therapy (HILT) with its biophysical effects, including photomechanical cellular stimulation, is an essential mechanism in tissue regeneration. This type of mechanical biostimulation is crucial in the management of cranial facial pain and includes promotion of hard and soft tissue regeneration. This level of biophysical effect on tissue can only be performed with a HILT laser and promotes cellular differentiation and proliferation for bone and cartilage regeneration. The utilization of DEKA’s HILT laser not only provides pain relief, it also improves bone remodeling and accelerates soft tissue healing and regeneration. Understanding what is happening in chronic and acute tissue conditions allows a clinician to better understand how to choose settings to provide a wide range of therapeutic benefits, it also aids in knowing when to modify treatment parameters.

rows.Event Name: Camp Congress for Youth Phoenix 2026
rows.Event Description: It's all civics & fun. Girls and boys study government, run for office, vote and convene a mock session of Congress to pass a bill. Camp Congress for Youth Phoenix 2026 is a leadership program that introduces girls and boys to the United States political system. Camp begins with a lesson on the structure of our political system. Each student will choose to run for a seat in the US House of Representatives, the US Senate or for the presidency. Students will create a campaign with a platform, campaign finance plan, and film a political ad. All campers will register to vote and then vote in an election. Once elected to Congress and sworn in, the newly installed members of Congress will learn how to introduce a bill, debate the merits of the bill, lobby fellow legislators, collaborate with the Executive branch and finally vote on the proposed bill. Camp will culminate with a ceremonial signing of the bill by our President. All participants will receive Certificate of Completion. For additional information contact us at Frequently Asked Questions Is this event a drop-off? All camps are drop-off unless parent participation is indicated. Parents may wait at the venue for the duration of the camp in any of the lounge or seating area throughout the hotel. The meeting room space capacity can only accommodate students and staff. Is the registration fee refundable? The registration fee is non-refundable unless the organizer cancels the session. If a participant is unable to attend a session a credit will be placed on file that may be used at any time for a future program. The credit does not expire. Is this a partisan program or supported by a political party? No, the Girls in Politics Initiative is an independent organization that is self-funded through the fees generated from hosting sessions. The curriculum is civic education. Is financial assistance available? Due to rising costs associated with hosting events, there is no financial assistance available for this event and there is no promo code for this event. Who are the instructors? Our Lead Instructors are women that currently or have previously worked in government or electoral politics. Our support staff are current or former licensed teachers. All instructors and support staff undergo background screening. All instructors and support staff are also in compliance with any local ordinances or state/provincial regulations applicable to those working with children.

rows.Event Name: Camp Congress for Girls Phoenix 2026
rows.Event Description: It's all fun & civics at Camp Congress for Girls Phoenix 2026. Girls study government, run for office & pass a bill. Camp Congress for Girls Phoenix 2026 is a leadership program that introduces girls to United States politics. Camp begins with a lesson on the structure of our political system. Each girl will choose to run for a seat in the US House of Representatives, the US Senate or for the presidency. She will create her own campaign with a platform, campaign finance plan, and film a political ad. All campers will register to vote and then vote in an election. Once elected to Congress and sworn in, the newly installed members of Congress will learn how to introduce a bill, debate the merits of the bill, lobby fellow legislators, collaborate with the Executive branch and finally vote on the proposed bill. Camp will culminate with a ceremonial signing of the bill by our Ms. President. All participants will receive Certificate of Completion. Participating Girl Scouts will earn a badge for completion of this session. Higher Hydration Mobile IV & Wellness will also be there offering specially priced vitamin shots and IV drips to keep you feeling your best. Enjoy some Chair Massage, Reiki, Tarot, and/or Light Frequency mini session add on options with are available before and after the experience. Wrap up with light refreshments and some quality time connecting with like-minded folks in a fun, relaxed atmosphere. Join us to recharge, reset, and reconnect — you deserve it! About them: What to Bring: All necessary supplies will be provided; however, you are welcome to bring your own mat, cozy blanket, pillows, or any personal items that make you feel comfortable. An eye mask, an intention, a journal are examples of optional items. Add on Option Pre-Registration Links: The combination of the above Ajna Light 3, Neuro Light, and Vibro-Acoustic System offers a powerful therapeutic approach to enhancing mental health. These technologies work together to stimulate the brain and body in ways that promote relaxation, reduce stress, and support emotional balance. The Ajna Light 3 uses pulsing light frequencies to synchronize brainwave activity, inducing states of deep relaxation and meditative awareness, which can help reduce symptoms of anxiety and depression. Similarly, the Neuro Light offers neurostimulation through light therapy, promoting positive neural activity and improving cognitive functions like focus and memory. These systems can help individuals break free from negative thought patterns and achieve mental clarity, which contributes to overall mental well-being. rows.Event Name: Dizgo
rows.Event Description: Dizgo w/ special guests Planck Born from attic jam sessions between childhood friends Andrew Pickel and Kevin Hinnefeld,Dizgo’s story began with two guitars, a tape recorder, and a dream-literally. The band’s namecame to Hinnefeld in a dream, before their first gig, reflecting the cosmic, boundary-breakingsound they’d soon deliver. Later joined by Jake Evatt on keys/vocals and Justin Clark on drums,the Bloomington-based quartet has since evolved into one of the country's premiere touringacts.Critics have called Dizgo “one of themost operative names in the jam band scene” and“Indiana’s psychedelic powerhouse,” blending the adventurous spirit of jamtronica with thedeep-pocket pulse of funk, the warmth of soul, explosive guitar, and the kaleidoscopic texturesof psychedelic rock.Their live shows are immersive journeys-a dance party and mind-triprolled into one. Interweaving soulful vocals, analog synths, and searing guitar complete withintricate compositions and extended improvisation. The result is something both danceable andintrospective: a dance floor where heads and hearts move in equal measure.Dizgo’s touring resume reads like a true music lover’s bucket list-standout sets at Peach MusicFestival, Summer Camp, Secret Dreams, Domefest and Sonic Bloom, alongside club andamphitheater performances supporting Goose, George Clinton & Parliament Funkadelic,lespecial, Dogs in a Pile, Pigeons Playing Ping Pong, and more. Their 2024 studio album “Melt”crystallized their ethos: when everything locks in, the band and the fans “melt into one collectiveunit,” channeling energy between themselves and the crowd in a feedback loop of groove.From your local venue to the biggest festival stage, Dizgo creates a communal experience-onewhere genre is merely a suggestion, the band andthe fans are a living organism, and everynote is a step further down the rabbit hole.

rows.Event Name: Healthy, Happy, and Bright: Kids Matter Wellness Program 2026
rows.Event Description: Empowering Local Children Through Fitness, Fun, and Healthy Living – Glendale, Arizona The While public systems play an essential role in healthcare and crisis response, preventive youth wellness education is often under-resourced and not delivered consistently in community settings. This program complements existing services by focusing on early intervention—equipping children with practical skills to manage stress, understand their emotions, and develop positive lifestyle behaviors before challenges escalate. Each year, the program reaches more than 300 children and engages families, community partners, and local organizations to create a collaborative approach to youth wellness. Through hands-on activities, education, and connection to resources, the Healthy, Happy, and Bright Wellness Program helps children grow into healthier, more confident, and emotionally resilient individuals.

rows.Event Name: Workplace Conflict Resolution Training in Phoenix, AZ
rows.Event Description: Master the Art of Conflict Management with Leadership Excellence. Conflict management is the process of identifying, addressing, and resolving disputes efficiently and respectfully to prevent worsening situations. It involves using techniques like active listening, open communication, and negotiation to find mutually agreeable solutions. Key strategies include collaborating, compromising, accommodating, avoiding, or competing, depending on the situation's urgency and importance. Conflicts are inevitable when people with different personalities and goals are put together to work in the same environment. Quite often, the clash results in extreme anger and animosity, and this can have a very disruptive effect on the entire team. As a leader, you are often called to defuse situations and resolve differences collaboratively before they spiral out of control. Our one-day Conflict Management and Communication Skills provide you with effective tools to manage stressful situations in the workplace and become a highly successful person and leader. On completion of this course, participants will be able to: When conflict is managed effectively, conflict can be viewed as a tool for exchanging ideas, ensuring that all parties are equally heard. "Teams cannot have effective communication without productive conflict." Adequately managed conflict is an effective form of communication rather than a fearful argument. The conflict management process involves steps such as identifying conflicts, analyzing conflict, exploring alternative solutions, applying conflict resolution techniques, selecting the best alternative, implementing the chosen solution, and reviewing its impact. The training workshop covers the following aspects: A provider of Enterprise Learning Solutions (ELS), Insight Learning creates industry-fit talents through training, coaching, and consulting by globally-acclaimed trainers. rows.Event Name: Guided Nidra Sound Bath at Dreamcatcher Healing Ranch
rows.Event Description: A guided Nidra and sound bath under the stars, paired with intentional connection time with horses. Step into a restorative evening under the stars designed to calm the nervous system, reconnect the body and mind, and gently attune through direct connection with horses. This guided Nidra sound bath takes place outdoors under the open desert sky, within the peaceful setting of Dreamcatcher Healing Ranch. Before the guided portion begins, guests are invited to spend intentional, grounded time with the horses, offering the opportunity for gentle interaction, touch, and presence. Horses are known for their ability to support nervous system regulation and emotional grounding, often reflecting calm through their steady, attuned nature. Once settled, guests will be guided into deep rest on luxury air mattresses prepared with bolsters, pillows, silky eye pillows, and cozy blankets. The experience begins with grounding vibration from large Tibetan bowls, followed by a guided Yoga Nidra practice designed to lead the body into a state of profound relaxation while the mind remains gently aware. The evening continues with an immersive sound bath featuring crystal bowls, gongs, chimes, and organic instruments that create wave-like tones to support release, rest, and nervous system regulation. Sound and stillness weave together to create a space where the body can soften and the mind can quiet naturally. Throughout the experience, thoughtful details support comfort and reflection, including grounding tea served after the session and a thank you gift to carry the inention beyond the evening. Guests are also welcome to spend additional quiet time with the horses This is more than a sound bath. It is a carefully curated, full-sensory experience designed to support rest, regulation, and reconnection in a natural, peaceful environment.

rows.Event Name: Master Your Mind, Elevate Your Performance, and Boost Your Energy Levels
rows.Event Description: Unlock the secrets to supercharging your life, achieving balance, and unleashing your true potential Do you feel like you're stuck in a rut and struggling to make progress in life? Are you constantly overwhelmed? If so, this event will change your life. Introducing "Master Your Mind, Elevate Your Performance, and Boost Your Energy Levels: The Ultimate Guide!" You'll get access to a transformative webinar and a private 60-minute coaching session valued at $145. And the best part? You'll get it all at a 70% discount! Imagine no longer feeling burdened by the weight of your responsibilities, wasting time and energy, and constantly being on the verge of burning out. With proven strategies, Tammy will show you how to supercharge your life and unlock your true potential. ✔ Experience freedom by taking control of your schedule, confidently declining unnecessary meetings, and empowering yourself with a positive mindset. ✔ Embrace the present moment, confidently assert your voice, and build deep, meaningful relationships with those who truly matter in your life. ✔ Say goodbye to procrastination and start achieving your goals by harnessing the power of focus, self-acceptance, and improved productivity. This is your opportunity to grab a co-driver on your journey to success. Let Tammy guide you from feeling like an old rusty transit van to become a supercharged, future-ready economic power machine! In just 22 minutes, you will have the tools and knowledge to bring balance into your life without compromising your success. Here's what you can expect: ✔ Discover the direction you want to travel in and improve your focus. ✔ Learn how to navigate life with grace and conviction, overcoming obstacles. ✔ Develop a bold and empowering self-image that goes beyond your current limitations. ✔ Boost your self-esteem and feel better about yourself, even when alone. ✔ Break free from the habit of overestimating tomorrow and start harnessing the power of today. ✔ Create a compelling vision for your life and use it as fuel for your daily actions. ✔ Identify gaps in your social circle and cultivate new empowering relationships. ✔ Embrace the notion that being selfish and prioritising your well-being is okay. ✔ Learn how to prepare for today to avoid unnecessary repairs in the future. After the webinar, you will receive a private 60-minute coaching session with a world-renowned personal development coach. In this personalised session, Tammy will work with you to craft a life plan tailored to your unique goals, with actionable steps you can implement immediately. This coaching session alone is valued at $145 , but as part of this limited-time offer, it's included with your webinar registration. Take advantage of this incredible opportunity to revolutionise your life. Join us for the event. Tammy is not only an entrepreneur and philanthropist but also an unwavering optimist who is dedicated to helping people overcome any obstacles that prevent them from achieving a balanced and successful life. From a young age, Tammy has always valued leadership while maintaining a healthy work-life balance. However, her journey to success was not without its challenges. Tammy has faced unemployment, heartaches, and embarrassing mistakes. Despite these setbacks, Tammy launched a successful business without substantial resources, built efficient systems, and grew an empowered team to achieve her goals. She now has the freedom to do more in less time. Tammy's innate abilities and lessons have empowered many to start, grow, or diversify their businesses or careers, enabling them to design fulfilling and purposeful lives.

rows.Event Name: Astrology 102:
rows.Event Description: Understanding Planetary Aspects How the Planets in Your Chart Communicate with Gena If you’ve already taken Astrology 101 or have a basic understanding of planets and signs, this next class opens the door to one of the most fascinating parts of astrology. Your planets don’t just sit quietly in your chart. They interact, influence, and “talk” to each other — creating patterns of energy that shape how your personality, emotions, gifts, and challenges express themselves. In this class, we’ll begin exploring those planetary conversations, learning how astrologers read the energetic relationships between planets and what those relationships can reveal about your life path. Rather than overwhelming you with complex formulas or memorization, we’ll approach aspects in a grounded, approachable way using the most familiar chart of all — your own. Expect a relaxed, welcoming environment where curiosity is encouraged and learning unfolds naturally. ✨ No pressure ✨ No complicated jargon ✨ Just another layer of your cosmic map being revealed ✨ What We’ll Explore Together 🌙 Quick recap from Astrology 101 We’ll briefly revisit planets, signs, and their meanings to help everyone feel grounded before we move forward. 🪐 What is an aspect? Understanding how astrologers measure the angles between planets and why these relationships matter. ✨ The Five Major Aspects Learning the primary planetary relationships: • Conjunction • Sextile • Square • Trine • Opposition These are the energetic connections that shape much of your chart’s story. 🌌 Minor Aspects An introduction to additional planetary relationships such as: • Semisquare • Semisextile • Sesquiquadrate • Quincunx • Quintile • Biquintile You’ll begin to see how these subtle influences add deeper nuance to a chart. ⚖️ Soft vs. Hard Aspects Understanding how different aspects carry different energetic tones — some flowing easily, others creating friction that pushes growth. 🔭 Orbs How close an angle needs to be for an aspect to count and how astrologers determine the strength of a planetary connection. 📚 Recommended Resources Helpful books and study tools for those who want to continue learning astrology beyond this class. 🌟 Reading a Planetary Conversation We’ll walk through a real chart example together and begin interpreting how planets communicate through aspects. 💫 Who This Class Is For This class is perfect for you if: ✨ You’ve taken Astrology 101 or understand the basics of planets and signs ✨ You’re curious about how astrologers interpret deeper chart patterns ✨ You want to begin understanding how your planetary energies interact ✨ You enjoy learning astrology in a relaxed, conversational environment 🌙 What You’ll Leave With By the end of this class, you’ll: ✨ Understand what planetary aspects are ✨ Recognize the major aspects in a birth chart ✨ Begin seeing how planets interact and influence each other ✨ Feel more confident exploring your own natal chart A space to connect, learn, grow, and expand your awareness of the cosmic language within you.

Use our paper, ink, and supplies to print your own posters, cards, coasters, and more... > Brief tour of our 6 presses, with demos and printed samples. We will show you how to operate these presses and samples of previous projects. Discover the analog world that still exists. > We will tour the studio and show you all of the prints from artists and printers around the world. Great opportunity to take pictures in front of our amazing poster walls. >Orientation regarding the type, images, and paper that are available to use. Check out our many type cabinets with metal and wood type that you will get to use to make your posters or other stationery projects. > Participants then set their own type (with ongoing instruction) and set the forms up to be printed. Get a little inky! We supply the aprons. > PRINTING! everyone can print their posters, coasters, or stationery. People often have several projects. You can print as many items as there is time for. > > Clean up - put things back where you got them, please. Other things to note: We have a 2 person minimum. No experience is required! Most of our guests have never done this before. It is good to wear older clothes, but, we have aprons. $60.00 per person – 2-hour workshop. Reservations required. Our workshops are for 2-6 people. Inquire about private group workshops or custom days and times! If you have a group of 4-10 people we will gladly set up a time and date of your choosing.

rows.Event Name: Youth Pottery Wheel Class (7+)
rows.Event Description: Get your hands dirty and unleash your creativity at our pottery wheel class! Ever wanted to try the pottery wheel? Learn the foundational steps to creating a vessel on the potter's wheel. This class is perfect for ages 7 through 18 to get a taste of the pottery process or to begin their pottery journey. You'll get a lot of support as you learn your way around the wheel in this small class setting. Classes are around 90 minutes long and limited to 6 people. Your teacher will share his wisdom and expertise as you shape 3 pieces of clay with your own hands while learning to center yourself through pottery. Afterwards, your created work will be glazed, fired, and ready for pick-up 3 weeks from the date of your class. Parents are encouraged to take this class with they kiddo and learn together! Asa Maestas is an experienced potter and instructor. He has been working with clay since 2011 and teaching pottery for over a decade. Asa found pottery in one of the darkest times in his life. As a young teenager, he started working with clay shortly after his mother passed. Working through this medium allowed him to work through loss and suffering as well as provided a means of self-expression. As he continued to discover, his work often consisted of a variety of textures applied in a loose pattern that used the entire surface of the clay, a style that continues to be a part of his artistic expression. Asa's passion for the art and craft of clay is a palpable part of the class. He is both a fun and educational teacher, eager to share his skill and expertise with you.

rows.Event Name: Somatic Dance Experience: Breathwork, Facial Somatics & Sound Healing
rows.Event Description: Release stress and reconnect to your body through Somatic Dance, breathwork, facial somatics, and sound healing for a full-body reset. This immersive somatic experience guides you through a sequence of healing modalities designed to help you unwind stored tension and regulate your nervous system. We begin with gentle breathwork to calm the mind and bring awareness back into the body. From there, you’ll be guided through facial somatics to release tension held in the face, jaw, and nervous system—areas often overlooked, yet deeply connected to stress, emotional patterns, and facial aging. You’ll then move into Somatic Dance, where you’ll be guided to move in a way that releases stuck energy, encourages expression, and reconnects you with your body in a safe, supportive space. No dance experience is needed. The experience closes with sound healing, allowing your body to fully integrate, relax, and reset from the inside out. Mats, pillows, and blankets will be provided, though you are welcome to bring your own for added comfort.

In this 3-hour, hands-on workshop, you’ll learn how to build and use a practical herbal first aid kit rooted in accessibility, effectiveness, and community care. This class is designed for beginners and those with some herbal experience alike, offering clear, grounded knowledge you can take into real-life situations. Together, we’ll explore how plant medicine can support the body through common first aid needs - from wound care and burns to digestive upset, pain, and nervous system regulation - and put these practices into your own kit. This class is not about perfection or having every herb on hand. It’s about learning how to work with a small, intentional set of plants in a way that is safe, responsive, and empowering. rows.Event Name: Data Analytics Certification Training in Tempe, AZ
rows.Event Description: Data Analytics is all about using data to make the right decisions. By Collecting, Cleaning, Ethics, Problem-Solving, and Analyzing Data. Data Analytics is the systematic process of collecting, cleaning, and analyzing raw data to discover meaningful patterns, trends, and actionable insights. By applying statistical analysis and technologies, it helps organizations make data-driven decisions to solve problems, boost efficiency, improve the customer experience, and predict future outcomes. This course will prepare learners to help organizations make informed business decisions and evaluate consumer behaviors and satisfaction, ultimately leading to the creation of improved products and services. The course syllabus is specifically designed to help learners acquire skills for setting achievable goals in the business field. This course is ideal for all working professionals without prior programming knowledge. It thoroughly covers essential topics such as data analytics, data visualization, and regression techniques. The Data Analystics certification course will equip you with the latest analytics tools and techniques, how to work with SQL databases and basic SQL queries, the programming languages, raw data manipulation, the art of creating data visualizations, and how to apply statistics and predictive analytics in a business environment. When considering Data Analytics, there are five essential elements you need to take into account: By the end, you’ll have a professional portfolio that you can show prospective employers or utilize for your own business. In this Data Analytics course, you will learn the latest analytics tools and techniques, the art of creating data visualizations, and how to apply statistics and predictive analytics in a business environment. Upon completing the Data Analytics course, you will have the data analysis skills necessary to get your dream job in the data analytics space. Apart from Data Analyst, other job titles include: Types of Data Analysis are Text, Statistical, Diagnostic, Predictive, and Prescriptive Analysis. Data Analysis consists of Data Requirement Gathering, Data Collection, Data Cleaning, Data Analysis, Data Interpretation, and Data Visualization. rows.Event Name: Monsters of the Desert: Arizona Cryptids & Local Lore

A towering creature seen along the Mogollon Rim. In this immersive Coffee Talk, we’ll explore the cryptids and folklore woven into Arizona’s history — the Mogollon Monster, Skinwalkers, La Llorona, the Chupacabra, the Red Ghost, and more. We’ll break down where these legends began, the cultural roots behind them, how they evolved, and why they still feel uncomfortably close in the Southwest. ☕ Enjoy the experience with Jarrod’s delicious vegan treats and handcrafted coffee as the stories unfold. Seating is limited. Come get Ghosted & Roasted with us!

rows.Event Name: Agile & Scrum Certification Training Program in Phoenix, AZ
rows.Event Description: Understanding the Dynamics of Modern Project Management with Agile and Scrum Concepts. Agile is an iterative, flexible project management philosophy focused on incremental delivery and continuous improvement, while Scrum is a specific, structured framework within Agile that uses fixed-length sprints to achieve goals. Agile provides the principles, whereas Scrum provides the concrete roles, artefacts, and ceremonies to implement them. Agile and Scrum are two versatile methodologies that have proven highly effective in transforming businesses, catalyzing innovation, and accelerating profitable growth. Scrum is the most dominant Agile framework, with a 58% adoption rate among organizations worldwide. Get introduced to the incredible concepts of Agile and Scrum with this beginner’s skills development course. Learn about the various Agile variants, including Scrum, Kanban, XP, and their tools. Master Agile Project Management (APM) and the 5-stage process for Agile software development before diving deep into Scrum. Understand how Agile is used in Project Development, including Agile Estimation, Planning, Monitoring, Tracking, and Agile metrics and tools. Leading organizations globally have adopted this powerful framework to improve time-to-market, boost quality, raise employee morale, and become truly adaptive. There are no eligibility requirements to attend the Agile and Scrum training. Everyone is welcome in the world of Agile and Scrum. A provider of Enterprise Learning Solutions (ELS), Insight Learning develops industry-fit talent through training, coaching, and consulting delivered by globally acclaimed trainers. Much of Insight Learning’s repute in co-creating business value stems from: Name: Jeffrey Adams Email: jeff.adams@insightlearnin.com Phone: +1 (713)-893-3306

rows.Event Name: Mountain Biking One-On-One Coaching
rows.Event Description: Get leveled-up faster with private mountain bike coaching. You say the place and time. Learn to conquer your favorite trail, or jump! P﻿rivate coaching gives you the best way to learn faster and ride better. You get immediate feedback on your riding. In these sessions you'll be shown examples and have them explained. You'll be given tailored instruction on how to break down maneuvers into parts so you can build up your skills to conquer your goals. T﻿hese sessions are great for one-on-one, parent/child pairs, and small groups. The coach will video you as you ride, then show you how to improve by reviewing video and giving you feedback. Chose a date that works for you. T﻿ext us at 417-592-6467 to confirm availability. F﻿inish your purchase here on Eventbrite. Our program uses techniques endorsed by PMBIA, the world's premier mountain biking instruction organization, coupled with teaching strategies of Brian K. Holder, M.A.T., Ed.S., PMBIA Lv1, a licensed and contracted teacher. WHAT TO BRING A well-tuned mountain bike Flat pedals, NOT “clip” or “clipless,” or clipless pedals with a platform. Please remove cages from flat pedals if so equipped. Closed-toe shoes that are compatible with the pedals you’ll be using. It’s important that your pedals and shoes work together. Appropriate dress for the climate conditions. Check the weather. Dress in layers if necessary. Bicycle helmet Protective gloves Hydration pack or water bottles Snacks (energy bars/gels/chews) Sunscreen Spare tube of appropriate size for your specific rim Basic repair tools (multi tool, tire levers, pump, etc.) A set of elbow and knee pads. Not required, but encouraged. Bug spray Glasses and/or sunglasses and case CANCELLATION POLICY Clinic registrations can either be transferred, or partially refunded per the following chart. If you need to make a change to your upcoming registration, please contact us right away. Weather In the event of rain or other inclement weather we will reschedule your event (season dependent) and/or provide a credit applicable to a future event. Satisfaction Guarantee We want you to be completely satisfied with your event. If you are dissatisfied in any way with the event you have attended, we’ll give you another opportunity, free of charge, to receive the skills instruction experience that meets your expectations. Why a clinic? MTB clinics focus on learning bike handling skills. The skills build on one another; a solid foundation of the basics is important. By learning the foundational skills one at time, a rider can feel comfortable and safe on unfamiliar, challenging terrain. Are the coaches certified? Yes. Coaches are PMBI (Professional Mountain Bike Instructor) certified and are certified in First Aid. What if there is something I am not comfortable with riding? Participation for everything is voluntary. Riders are NOT required to do anything that makes them uncomfortable or is above their skill level. If the coach feels the rider isn't experienced to be in the clinic he or she signed up for, the coach has the right for the safety of the rider to send them home. L﻿IABILITY WAIVER A﻿ll participants will be required to sign a release of liability waiver before the clinic begins.

rows.Event Description: Handmade Empanadas Join us for an evening in Argentina, where you will learn to make the flaky meets juicy pastry pocket we all know and love: The Empanada. These flavorful patties made of dough are traditionally filled with meat, seafood, cheese, vegetables or fruit, and have a variety of adaptions based on the country you enjoy them in. In this fun and hands-on class, you will be guided step-by-step in preparing your own Empanadas from scratch with authentic Argentine-style fillings. You will learn to make the dough to then fill with a savory ground beef and spices, as well as a sweet apple and cinammon filling. Next, the Chef will demonstrate how to fill your empanada disks before folding and sealing the edges off to be baked to a flaky, golden perfection. The best part of the class will be sitting down to enjoy your delicious made from scratch meal, with new-found skills to remake this special dish and share with friends and family back home!No experience is required! Welcome to Thyrd Period, a NEW boozy lecture series by Thyrd Space where curious minds gather for connection, conversation, and a splash of philosophy after hours. For this three-part series ( Across three sessions, Dr. Henson will guide us through abolition’s historical roots, the debates surrounding it, and what it asks us to reconsider about justice, harm, and accountability. Each gathering blends a short lecture with interactive elements, small-group discussion, and reflection. This isn’t your typical lecture. Expect thoughtful ideas, a relaxed atmosphere, and plenty of room to engage with others who enjoy learning together—drink in hand (mocktails welcome) and snacks nearby. Come curious💡 Leave thoughtful 💭 Questions? Please reach out to us at Follow us on Instagram Through a short lecture, reflection prompts, and conversation with fellow guests, we’ll start unpacking the assumptions many of us hold about justice, punishment, and accountability. Along the way, we’ll ask questions like: Guided by Dr. Henson’s Eight Acknowledgements of Abolition, this session builds on the first four acknowledgements introduced in the opening lecture and turns to the remaining four. Together, these ideas deepen the conversation by inviting us to critically examine how we define safety, harm, and well-being. Through reflection and discussion, we’ll explore how these acknowledgements challenge conventional assumptions and expand the ways we might think about accountability, care, and community. Our final session turns toward the future. After exploring the ideas that ground the abolitionist agenda, we’ll shift to what this looks like in practice. This session highlights real-world abolitionist practices already happening in communities today—many of which may look different than you think. Together, we’ll consider what these approaches reveal about building safety, responding to harm, and imagining new possibilities for justice moving forward. Thyrd Space gatherings are designed to be welcoming, curious, and respectful environments for dialogue. By attending, you agree to treat speakers, staff, and fellow attendees with respect and follow all venue guidelines. Disruptive or unsafe behavior may result in removal from the event.

rows.Event Description: Join thousands across North America in 30+ cities to walk and raise awareness for Save Soil! Over 52% of the world’s farmable soil is degraded, contributing to nutritional imbalances for 2 billion people. The next decade is critical to reversing this crisis—together we can bring awareness about soil health and secure the future of our children and our planet. Save Soil is a The movement is working with governments across the globe to shape soil policies, and is supporting over Save Soil is backed by the UN Environment Program, UN Food and Agriculture Organisation, UN Convention to Combat Desertification, World Food Program, and IUCN, amongst others. Yogi, Mystic and Visionary, Sadhguru is among the most influential voices of our times. A New York Times bestselling author, Sadhguru is acclaimed as a speaker and opinion-maker of international renown. His videos, which stream in 35 languages on social media, were viewed more than 4.37 billion times in 2023 alone. He is the only living Indian to have received three Presidential Awards from three different Presidents, among which are India’s highest annual civilian award, the Padma Vibhushan, and India’s highest environmental award, the Indira Gandhi Paryavaran Puraskar. As part of the Conscious Planet initiative, he has also launched the world’s largest people’s movement – Save Soil, which has so far reached over 4.1 billion people and was greatly instrumental in bringing soil, agriculture, and food systems to the forefront. rows.Event Name: Golden Hour Yin Yoga
rows.Event Description: Long-held poses, breathwork, and stillness to support deep relaxation and fascia release. All levels welcome. Yin Yoga is a slow, meditative style of yoga that targets the deep connective tissues of the body — including the fascia, ligaments, joints, and bones. Unlike more dynamic styles of yoga, Yin poses are held for extended periods, typically 3 to 5 minutes, allowing the body to gradually release tension and increase flexibility at a deeper level. In a Yin Yoga class, you’ll spend most of the time close to the ground in seated or reclined postures, supported by stillness and your breath. The practice encourages you to soften muscular effort and surrender into each pose, cultivating patience, mindfulness, and a deep sense of calm. Yin Yoga is suitable for all levels and is particularly beneficial for those looking to: • Improve joint mobility and flexibility • Relieve chronic tension and stress • Complement more active (Yang) movement practices • Develop a deeper mind-body connection • Support recovery and relaxation Whether you’re a seasoned yogi or a complete beginner, Yin Yoga offers a grounding, restorative experience that nourishes both body and mind.
